⚖

botl

Run Claude Code in sandboxed Docker containers

Open Source MIT License

The Problem

Claude Code runs on your machine with access to your entire filesystem, credentials, and network.

You want AI help on a repo — without the risk.

The Solution

botl wraps Claude Code inside an ephemeral Docker container that self-destructs on exit.

  • ✓ All mounts are read-only
  • ✓ Container drops all capabilities
  • ✓ Nothing persists unless you say so

How It Works

🔧 Build
➔
▶ Run
➔
🤖 Claude works
➔
📋 Review
# Build the sandbox image (once)
$ botl build

# Launch Claude on any repo
$ botl run https://github.com/you/repo

Two Ways to Run

🗣 Interactive
# Opens a live Claude session
$ botl run \
  https://github.com/you/repo

# You chat with Claude directly
# inside the container
⚡ Headless
# Fire-and-forget with a prompt
$ botl run \
  https://github.com/you/repo \
  -p "fix all lint errors"

# Claude works autonomously

Run Options

# Specific branch + 1-hour timeout
$ botl run https://github.com/you/api \
  -b feat/auth --timeout 1h

# Extra mount + env variable
$ botl run https://github.com/you/api \
  -m /data/fixtures:/fixtures \
  -e DATABASE_URL=postgres://...

# Deep clone (full git history)
$ botl run https://github.com/you/api \
  --clone-mode deep --blocked-ports 8080,3000

Key Features

📦

Package Caching

Auto-mounts Go, Node, Python, Rust caches read-only

🔒

Port Blocking

Block inbound ports via iptables inside the container

⚡

Shallow Cloning

Fast depth-1 clones by default, full history optional

🔖

Reusable Profiles

Save session configs and replay with --with-label

Security: Container Hardening

🚫
--cap-drop ALL
All Linux capabilities dropped. Only SETUID, SETGID, CHOWN, FOWNER re-added for setup.
🔒
Read-only mounts
Host packages, credentials, extra mounts — all :ro. Only output dir is writable.
👤
Non-root execution
Entrypoint runs as root for setup, then drops to unprivileged botl user via gosu.
💣
Ephemeral containers
--rm flag destroys container on exit. Nothing persists unless you export.
🔐
Credential isolation
Only auth tokens copied (not plugin caches). Files re-owned to botl user with 0600.
🔭
Git history sanitization
Shallow mode purges reflog, runs gc --prune=now, strips git notes.

Security: Configuration

# Block specific ports (iptables REJECT inside container)
$ botl run https://github.com/you/api \
  --blocked-ports 8080,3000,5432

# Or set as persistent default
$ botl config set blocked-ports 8080,3000

# Deep clone (keep full git history)
$ botl run https://github.com/you/api \
  --clone-mode deep

# Disable auto-mounting host packages
$ botl run https://github.com/you/api \
  --mount-packages=false

NET_ADMIN capability is only added when port blocking is active. Input validation rejects file:// URLs, path traversal, and BOTL_ env prefix.

Configuration

Persistent defaults in ~/.config/botl/config.yaml

# View all settings
$ botl config list

# Set defaults
$ botl config set clone-mode deep
$ botl config set blocked-ports 8080,3000

# Read a single value
$ botl config get clone-mode
deep

Labels & Profiles

Save a session's config as a named profile, then reuse it for any repo.

# After a run, save its config as a profile
$ botl label a3f2c1d8 secure-api
✓ Profile saved

# Reuse it on any repo
$ botl run https://github.com/you/other-api \
  --with-label secure-api

# Manage profiles
$ botl profiles list
$ botl profiles show secure-api
$ botl profiles delete secure-api

When Claude Finishes

A TUI menu lets you choose what happens next:

1 Push to a remote branch (default: botl/<timestamp>)
2 Export a git diff patch to ./botl-output
3 Save the entire workspace directory
4 Discard everything and exit cleanly

Get Started

$ go install github.com/kamilrybacki/botl@latest
$ botl build
$ botl run https://github.com/you/repo

Requires Go 1.24+, Docker, and a Claude Pro/Max subscription.

github.com/kamilrybacki/botl
